description: Build a cluster-interaction matrix W (W[i,j] = number of bridge theorems linking clusters i,j); per cluster compute integration (entropy of its outgoing bridge distribution) x differentiation (1 - normalized module entropy) = a Tononi-Edelman-inspired neural complexity CN.
motivation: Quantify the modular-yet-integrated structure of mathematics and identify its most integrative-yet-specialized domain.
formal_results: Global CN 1.167; top cluster SetTheory CN=1.037 (integration 2.61 x differentiation 0.397); next Algebra+RingTheory+FieldTheory 0.495, Order 0.388; CategoryTheory high integration but low differentiation (0.307).
results_description: SetTheory is by far the most integrative-yet-specialized cluster.
conclusions: By a Tononi-Edelman-inspired complexity measure, SetTheory is the dominant integrative hub of Mathlib - both broadly connected and distinctly specialized. (The CN measure is heuristic/exploratory, not the exact Tononi-Edelman summation formula.)

comments.caveat: CN = integration x differentiation is a Tononi-Edelman-INSPIRED heuristic, not the exact summation formula; the paper flags it as exploratory with formal justification left to future work.
